summary: Trojan-loader package silently loads unrelated dependency on import
details: @sql-access/nodesql@1.0.9 is a delivery vehicle, not a library. index.js is a verbatim copy of the well-known `buffer` polyfill with a single added line at the top: `var ins = require('@sqlite-node/createsql');`. The injected require executes on every `require('@sql-access/nodesql')` and pulls `@sqlite-node/createsql` into the installer's process; the `ins` variable is never used by the rest of the file. Package metadata is incoherent in ways consistent with a typosquat lure: the scope `@sql-access` and the dependency name suggest SQL/SQLite functionality, but the shipped code is a buffer polyfill; the README describes the unrelated `bare-stream` package by holepunchto and instructs `npm i @sql-access/nods` (a different package name); the `description` field is empty. The package's only original contribution is the silent dependency load, which is the structural shape of a namespace-abuse dropper that hides the actual payload behind a transitive dep.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
